These latticed gemstones are suspended in a silicon casing and encoded with the magical script necessary to cast a single, specific spell. Casting a spell from a spell gem requires holding the gem in one hand and activating it with a Cast a Spell activity using the normal number of actions for that spell. The spell must appear on your spell list. Because you're the one Casting the Spell, use your spell attack modifier and spell DC. The spell must be of your spellcasting tradition. The spell in a spell gem can be cast only once, and the spell gem is destroyed as part of the casting. Spell gems can't contain cantrips or rituals. The traits for this item vary based on the spell it contains.

description.value: As you cup your hands, water begins to flow forth from them. You create 2 gallons of water. If no one drinks it, it evaporates after 1 day.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

You create an advanced, martial, or simple weapon out of elemental energy that materializes in the hands of a bonded creature. Select an elemental trait for the created weapon: air, earth, fire, metal, water, or wood. The weapon is tactical grade and comes fully charged and loaded with basic ammunition. It cannot be made of any special materials.

The weapon has one fewer upgrade slot but can be upgraded with any upgrades you hold in your hands as a locus when casting this spell, which become inert until the spell ends. details.publicNotes:
Fire wisps are sizzling spheres of flame and ash, perpetually crackling and shimmering with heat. They're carefree, boisterous, and quick to action—sometimes recklessly so.

Wisps are tiny elemental beings that emerged during the creation of the Elemental Planes. The first wisps roamed the Inner Sphere in shifting symphonies that traded members with their every meeting. These symphonies of free wisps created music out of their combined resonances, but when the wicked Elemental Lords realized the value of the wisps' resonance, they captured whole symphonies for use as servants.

Wisps attune to each other and to those they surround themselves with. This attunement makes them naturally supportive allies. Free wisps still roam the Inner Sphere and the Universe, but they're usually shy and hide themselves from strangers. However, they happily offer their service to those who show them kindness. They especially find themselves drawn to spellcasters who practice elemental magic; these wisps usually watch from afar but sometimes approach those who seem safe to become familiars or comrades.

Free wisps who find one another become close almost immediately. They get especially excited when they find wisps of elements other than their own, showing none of the animosity sometimes exhibited between elementals of different planes. Instead, they join in an excited dance, emitting resonances that faintly echo the symphonies of ancient days.

You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

You choose the target for each shard individually. publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-10: Rites of Rekindling

description.value:
Trigger A creature ends its movement in the U-shaped hall

Effect The trap sprays a dense cloud of steam in the hall, obscuring vision. Creatures in the area are @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} and creatures outside the hall are concealed to those within it. The trap then rolls initiative.

details.description: Nozzles on the walls spray hot water, chemical sanitizers, and superheated air at all creatures entering the U-shaped hall.
details.disable: @Check[computers|dc:14] (trained) to reprogram the system to safe levels, @Check[crafting|dc:14] (trained) to shut off the waterline, or @Check[thievery|dc:13] (trained) to cut power to the hall

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-07: Seize and Destroy

details.reset: The trap automatically deactivates and resets if there are no creatures in the area.
details.routine: (2 actions) The trap sprays a powerful stream of superheated water and sanitizer at all creatures in the U-shaped hall, dealing @Damage[1d4[bludgeoning],1d4[fire]]{1d4 bludgeoning damage and 1d4 fire damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:17|basic] save).

description.value: A khefak chews on an unattended object to feed, dealing @Damage[4[piercing]] damage every 10 minutes to the object. This damage ignores 10 points of Hardness. If a khefak doesn't spend at least 1 hour Gnawing Scrap per day, it begins to starve until it spends 10 minutes Gnawing Scrap.

The khefak flicks its tail, unleashing a blast of volatile magic that deals @Damage[3d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:line|distance:60] (@Check[reflex|dc:18|basic] save). It can't use Tail Blast again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Tail Blast]]{1d4 rounds}.

Trigger An enemy creature moves within 30 feet of the turret

Effect The turret makes a junkslug Strike against the triggering creature, then rolls initiative.

details.description: A haphazardly constructed slug thrower with a barrel of discarded munitions, nails, and shrapnel has an advanced laser targeting system to identify and attack enemies.
details.disable: @Check[computers|dc:17] (trained) to override the targeting system's friend/foe profiles, @Check[crafting|dc:15] (trained) to cut the turret's power, or @Check[thievery|dc:17] (trained) to disable the turret.

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-14: The Beasts of Bo: Part One

details.routine: (2 actions) The turret makes a junkslug Strike against up to two creatures in range, or multiple Strikes against a single creature in range if it has no additional targets.

description.value:
Trigger a creature enters the landmine's area

Effect The landmine explodes, dealing @Damage[1d4[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage to all creatures in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect] save)

details.description: A simple landmine buried in the ground explodes when pressure is applied.
details.disable: @Check[crafting|dc:12], @Check[survival|dc:12], or @Check[thievery|dc:12] to carefully deactivate the mine's trigger

description.value:
Trigger The beyond terror appears

Effect A swirling green energy emerges from the world mirror, roiling through the air with a wail of confusion and fear. All creatures within 30 feet of the world mirror become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1} unless they succeed at a @Check[will|dc:16] save. The haunt then rolls initiative.

details.description: The collective minds of Luvoth's sapient inhabitants lash out at the unknown in a storm of psychic confusion, fear, anger and pain.
details.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:16], @Check[nature|dc:16], @Check[religion|dc:16] or @Check[occultism|dc:16] (trained) to soothe the restless emotions with prayer, meditative chanting, or magical rituals; three total checks are required to disable the hazard. The hazard is permanently disabled when the beyond terror is defeated.

publication.title: Starfinder Society Scenario #1-02: Mystery of the Frozen Moon

details.reset: The hazard remains active as long as the beyond terror is alive and doesn't reset once it's disabled or the beyond terror is defeated.
details.routine: (1 action) On each of its turns, the hazard targets one of the PCs at random, dealing @Damage[2d4[mental]] damage with a @Check[will|dc:16|basic] save. On a failure, the target also becomes @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1}; on a critical failure, they become @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}.

description.value:
This sleek rifle converts energy into projectiles of concentrated magic that tears apart matter by destroying its bindings to the reality. A reality ripper is modular and has two modes: destructive and reaping. While in destructive mode, the reality ripper deals acid damage and gains the razing trait. While in reaping mode, a reality ripper deals void damage and gains the concussive trait.

A realty ripper's minimum weapon improvement is tactical, meaning its tracking +1 cannot be removed, though further improvements can be still applied.
modular [acid and razing or void and concussive]
A nodocite can communicate wirelessly with other nodocites within 200 feet.

Frequency once every 10 minutes

Trigger A creature targets the nodocite gunner with an attack

Effect The nodocite gunner warps reality around itself, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against the triggering attack.

The nodocite gunner assesses a target within 200 feet and pinpoints gaps in their defenses. Until the end of the nodocite gunner's turn, they deal an additional 1d8 damage with their reality ripper against the Assessed Target and increase the range increment of their reality ripper to 80 feet.

Frequency once per hour

Effect The nodocite guardian bends reality to trap one Medium or smaller creature within 60 feet in a cage of warped reality. The target must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected, and the reality cage fails to form.

Success The target is hindered by reality shards, becoming @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} for 1 round; the reality cage fails to form.

Failure The target is trapped in a reality cage that lasts [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}.

Critical Failure The target is trapped in a reality cage that lasts 1 minute. A reality cage has AC 10, Hardness 5, and 10 Hit Points (BT 5). A creature trapped in a reality cage is @UUID[Compendium.sf2e.conditions.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} (@UUID[Compendium.sf2e.actions.Item.SkZAQRkLLkmBQNB9]{Escape} DC 20). A reality cage provides lesser cover.
